data.title: Meta tests 50-day moving average following an early morning pop
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) saw an early morning surge that briefly pushed the mega-cap stock above its 50-, 100-, and 200-day moving averages. The initial pop lifted shares by more than 6% following a report that CEO Mark Zuckerberg plans to scale back metaverse projects by up to 30%.

This marks a notable rebound, as Meta had last closed above its 50-day moving average on October 29, before sliding lower in the wake of its most recent earnings report. Currently, the stock is hovering near its 50-day moving average, appearing to hold just below this key momentum level.

Within the broader Magnificent Seven group of mega-cap tech names, only Alphabet (GOOG)(GOOGL), Apple (AAPL), and Tesla (TSLA) are trading above their respective 50-day moving averages. In contrast, Nvidia (NVDA), Microsoft (MSFT), Amazon (AMZN), and Meta Platforms (META) remain below this important technical threshold, reflecting ongoing pressure despite intermittent rebounds. 

Market participants will be closely watching whether META can sustain momentum above its 50-day line, a critical indicator for potential near-term recovery, while the rest of the group continues to navigate mixed technical signals amid a recent broader tech market rally that now has the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) just 2.4% away from its all-time trading high. 

Additional Data on META

  • 1-Month: +4.2%
  • 6-Month: -0.2% 
  • Year-to-Date: +13.6%
  • SA Quant Rating: 3.31
  • SA Analyst Rating: 4.05
  • Wall Street Rating: 4.61

Magnificent Seven Focused ETFs: (MAGS), (MAGX), (QQQU), and (QQQD).

data.title: The comeback at Playboy continues as shares rally to a ten-month high
data.description:
Playboy (PLBY) shot up 11.1% in late morning trading on Thursday to trade at its highest level since February.

The company continues to make notable progress with its transition to an asset-light, content & license business. Playboy (PLBY) handed off operational control of key adult properties (Playboy Plus, Playboy TV, creator platform) to Byborg under a long-term license with minimum guaranteed royalties. That structure shifts the regulatory and compliance burden to Byborg while Playboy primarily earns brand and content royalties, along with a profit-sharing component.

Roth Capital sees more upside for Playboy (PLBY) due to its pipeline of license/content initiatives that are in development but not yet reflected in our current estimates

A minor tailwind in the future for Playboy (PLBY) could be that many U.S. states have recently passed or implemented age-verification laws that force porn sites to verify users’ identities with government IDs or third-party services, causing some large platforms to block users in those states instead of complying. The rationale is that Playboy's (PLBY) licensing deals could be more attractive if consumer interest in its licensed content increases.

Looking ahead, Playboy (PLBY) will be making an appearance at the Roth Capital Partners 14th Annual Deer Valley Event on December 10-13.

data.title: Strategy partners with HCLTech to scale Mosaic deployment
data.description:
Strategy (MSTR) is teaming up with tech company HCLTech to accelerate the global adoption and deployment of its AI-powered Mosaic offering for enterprise clients, the firms said Thursday.

Mosaic is a "universal semantic layer" that sits on top of a company's data to help systems better understand and use that data.

As part of the partnership, Strategy (MSTR) has named HCLTech a preferred enterprise solution and professional services partner. The collaboration is set to provide clients a comprehensive suite of solutions to integrate Mosaic into complex, multicloud environments at scale.

Strategy (MSTR) is better known for being the world's largest corporate holder of bitcoin (BTC-USD). As of Nov. 30, 2025, the Michael Saylor-founded company held a total of 650K BTC.

data.title: BP in advanced talks to sell $8B Castrol unit to Stonepeak - FT
data.description:
U.S. infrastructure investor Stonepak has emerged as the leading contender to acquire BP's (BP) Castrol lubricants unit, as the Financial Times reported Thursday it is in advanced talks over an offer that would value the business at more than $8 billion.

BP (BP) put Castrol up for sale in February, with analysts suggesting it could fetch more than $10 billion - which would bring the company halfway to its goal of raising $20 billion from asset sales by 2027 - but initial bidders balked at that price, and a deal for Castrol at a valuation of above $8 billion would match BP's current expectations, according to the report.

Stonepak reportedly could seek a deal similar in structure to its recent purchase of a majority stake in a subsidiary of Phillips 66, which retained a 35% minority interest.

BP (BP), which also is trying to find a buyer for half of its Lightsource solar business, has said it expects to raise $4 billion from disposals this year.

data.title: U.S. stocks struggle for direction after opening gains peter out
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages seesawed on Thursday as investors assessed Meta Platforms' (META) rise after Mark Zuckerberg is reportedly cutting Metaverse spending, and jobless claims came lower than expected.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last -0.1% in midday trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.1%, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.1%.

Meta Platforms (META) -- last +4% -- rose as Bloomberg reported that Mark Zuckerberg said that the company is looking at cutting Metaverse efforts by up to 30%.

On the economic side, the initial jobless claims retreated to below 200K and were better than expected. U.S.-based employers announced 71.32K job cuts in November, down 53% from the 153.07K announced in October.

“Initial claims collapsed last week to a three-year low. The Bureau of Labor Statistics, which compiles this report, did not note any special factors. So, as I highlighted in red all year, the less-than-5-day workweeks distort this series,” said Jim Bianco, president and macro strategist at Bianco Research.

“Last week was Thanksgiving. Previously, these short workweeks resulted in a spike in claims, but Thanksgiving week saw a collapse. Even if you adjust for this week for Thanksgiving, the data still doesn't suggest the Fed should cut rates next week,” he added.

In other news, President Donald Trump's aides were reportedly in talks about appointing Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent as the top White House economic adviser, in addition to his current role, if Kevin Hassett is picked as the next Federal Reserve chair.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 3 basis points to 4.10%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 3 basis points to 3.53%.

data.title: Genesco outlines revised $0.95 EPS guidance amid Schuh headwinds and Journeys momentum
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Genesco Inc. (GCO) Q3 2026

Management View

  • Mimi Vaughn, Chairman, President & CEO, reported, “We delivered solid performance versus last year in this important back-to-school quarter led by Journeys, which achieved 6% comp growth, and more than a 50% increase in operating income.” Vaughn noted this profitability was partially offset by “the anticipated exit of licenses in Genesco Brands Group, the impact of tariffs, and more than expected gross margin pressure at Schuh as the U.K. market faced heightened promotional activity.” She highlighted that “total comparable sales increased 3%, with store comps up a noteworthy 5%, coupled with a modest decline in e-commerce comps.”
  • Vaughn stated, “Journeys continues executing against its strategic plan to accelerate growth, delivering its fifth consecutive quarter of positive comp growth along with almost 200 basis points of operating margin expansion.” She emphasized meaningful market share gains for Journeys and the traction of new initiatives. Vaughn further described the launch of the Life on Loud campaign, surpassing 70 million social views, and the formation of the Journeys Global Retail Group under Andy Gray, uniting Journeys, Schuh, and Little Burgundy. This new structure aims to leverage synergies and improve Schuh’s performance.
  • Vaughn announced the introduction of Peyton Manning as Johnston & Murphy’s brand ambassador, which generated “an immediate double-digit traffic increase.” She also discussed the Levi’s license wind down and the planned Wrangler footwear launch for fall '26.
  • Cassandra Harris, Senior VP of Finance, CFO & Principal Accounting Officer, stated, “Total revenue for the quarter was $616 million, up 3% compared to last year, driven by overall comparable sales growth of 3%, reflecting positive 6% comps at Journeys and 2% lower comps at Schuh and J&M.” Harris added, “Gross margin for the quarter was 46.8%, down 100 basis points from last year.” She highlighted improvements in SG&A expense, which was “44.7% of sales leveraging 140 basis points year-over-year.”

Outlook

  • Vaughn indicated, “We have materially changed our sales and margin projections for Schuh, to reflect the ongoing difficult U.K. market and performance. We have also moderated the growth assumptions for our other businesses based on the footwear, consumer traffic, and spending patterns we’ve witnessed on nonpeak shopping days.”
  • Harris provided updated guidance, stating, “We now expect to deliver full year adjusted earnings per share of approximately $0.95, reflecting a higher tax rate of 34%.” She emphasized ongoing gross margin pressure at Schuh and more conservative sales assumptions across the portfolio, but maintained expectations for mid-single-digit comp growth at Journeys.

Financial Results

  • Harris reported, “Adjusted operating income for the quarter was $12.9 million, above last year’s $10.3 million, resulting in adjusted diluted earnings per share of $0.79 compared to $0.61 in the same period last year.” She noted improved free cash flow and clean inventory levels, with capital expenditures totaling $18 million. The company ended the quarter with 1,245 stores, including 76 Journeys 4.0 locations, and expects more than 80 by year-end. No shares were repurchased in the quarter, leaving $29.8 million under the current authorization.

Q&A

  • Mitchel Kummetz, Seaport Research Partners: “If my math is close to being right, that implies maybe sort of flat to down low single-digit sales for Journeys in the fourth quarter. I’m wondering if that’s essentially what you’re thinking... Are you kind of looking for sort of a flat to slightly positive comp for Journeys in Q4?” Vaughn responded, “We are, again, optimistic for positive comps for Journeys. We have closed some stores, and so that ends up impacting the overall sales number. But in fact, Journeys is more productive in the stores that we are running, and we are using initiatives like the 4.0 to make the strong stores even stronger.”
  • Kummetz: “Can you say what [the lift from 4.0 stores] was in the third quarter?” Vaughn answered, “We continue to see about the same level of performance out of the 4.0s, just really strong overall performance. And even during the nonpeak periods, our 4.0 stores performed at a higher level than the rest of our store fleet.”
  • Kummetz: “Was Journeys positive comp in November?” Vaughn replied, “We were positive with Journeys comps in November. We saw a strengthening into the holiday. We had a record Black Friday on top of a record Black Friday last year.”
  • Joseph Civello, Truist Securities: “Can you give any more color on the demand trends between Canvas and Athletic? One is the divergence between the 2 expanding?” Vaughn explained that athletic styles are seeing more year-round growth, while canvas remains “an important part of our mix,” but with less demand. Vaughn highlighted, “We are really excited about the lifestyle athletic that we are selling.”
  • Mantero Moreno-Cheek, Jefferies LLC: “Are there any major next steps to continue expanding Journeys’ brand portfolio?” Vaughn responded that the company is “absolutely pursuing a strategy of more diversification,” citing recent introductions like Nike, HOKA, and Saucony, and emphasizing the importance of having the right brands to manage consumer rotation in preferences.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst tone was probing and slightly cautious, focusing on sustainability of Journeys’ comp growth, margin headwinds, and the trajectory of key initiatives, as seen in repeated requests for clarification and future expectations.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in prepared remarks and reiterated optimism for Journeys and strategic initiatives, but acknowledged challenges, especially at Schuh. Vaughn stated, “We are optimistic for positive comps for Journeys,” while also noting, “we have materially changed our sales and margin projections for Schuh.”
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone shifted from confident to more cautious regarding Schuh and overall margin outlook, while analysts remained consistent in their focus on key business drivers.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance was lowered from an adjusted EPS range of $1.30-$1.70 in Q2 to approximately $0.95 for the year, primarily due to increased pressure at Schuh and more conservative sales assumptions across the portfolio.
  • Journeys’ store comps and performance remained strong, with mid-single-digit comp growth still expected. However, Schuh faced greater-than-anticipated margin and sales pressure due to the U.K. promotional environment.
  • Management’s sentiment in the current quarter reflected increased caution, particularly regarding Schuh and margin trends, whereas the previous quarter’s tone was more optimistic about sustaining momentum.
  • Analysts in both quarters continued to probe around growth drivers, margin pressures, and new initiatives, with increased attention this quarter on the sustainability of Journeys’ performance and the impact of the promotional U.K. market.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified heightened promotional activity and weak consumer demand in the U.K. as primary challenges for Schuh, as well as ongoing tariff pressures.
  • Vaughn stated, “We have materially changed our sales and margin projections for Schuh, to reflect the ongoing difficult U.K. market and performance.”
  • The wind-down of the Levi’s license and continued liquidation in Genesco Brands Group presented onetime headwinds; management expects liquidation to be completed by year-end.
  • Management is taking actions to rightsize inventory, implement targeted marketing, and optimize store fleets to mitigate these risks.

Final Takeaway

Genesco’s Q3 2026 results showcased continued momentum at Journeys with strong comp growth and major new brand launches, such as Nike, driving higher in-store productivity. However, the outlook for the full year was revised downward due to increased margin and sales pressure at Schuh amid a highly promotional U.K. market and ongoing tariff headwinds. Management reaffirmed its confidence in Journeys’ strategic plan and brand-building initiatives, while taking proactive steps to address short-term challenges in other segments. The company remains focused on disciplined execution and flexibility to position itself for long-term value creation.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Figure Technology launches real-world assets consortium, shares up
data.description:
Figure Technology (FIGR) stock was surging as the blockchain-native capital marketplace announced the launch of a real-world assets consortium.

Shares were +9.78% Thursday morning to $38.84.

The consortium represents a coordinated effort by crypto platforms to support the adoption of PRIME, a liquid staking token built on the Hastra liquidity protocol.

The consortium, consisting of Figure, Kamino Finance, Chainlink, CASH, Raydium, Privy, and Gauntlet, aims to deliver the first full-stack framework for the PRIME token on Solana.

Users can access PRIME tokens at hastra.io and participate in borrow-lend markets through Kamino Finance.

"For the first time, a DeFi user with $100 can participate in the same loan pools as major financial institutions, earning yields from real lending activity with full transparency and instant liquidity," said Mike Cagney, the executive chairman of Figure.

data.title: Small businesses continue to struggle amid a K-shaped economy – Pantheon Macroeconomics
data.description:
The U.S. economy is experiencing a stark bifurcation with continued growth in the AI sector (ARTY), (AIQ), (AIEQ) and strong high-end consumer spending contrasting sharply with struggling small businesses and mass-market consumers, according to Pantheon Macroeconomics chairman Ian Shepherdson.

In an interview with CNBC, Shepherdson characterized the current economic landscape as “K-shaped,” where headline economic numbers reflect this split reality rather than uniform growth or decline.

“The AI boom continues. The high-end consumer is doing great, but the mass market consumer and the small business guys are not doing great,” he said.

He cautioned against overreacting to volatile monthly economic indicators, particularly during holiday seasons when data can be skewed by seasonal adjustments.

Small businesses, which employ approximately half of the American workforce, face significant headwinds from tariffs and policy uncertainty. Shepherdson emphasized that while tariffs are manageable, albeit expensive and time-consuming, it’s the unpredictability about future economic policy that is particularly damaging.

“It’s what’s going to happen next? How do I plan? How do I allocate my resources, my capital?” he explained, noting that this uncertainty has led to reduced capital expenditure and hiring plans.

Looking ahead, Shepherdson predicted a positive economic bump in spring 2026, driven by substantial tax refunds.

“You’re going to get a big tax refund season… some of the estimates are as high as 1% of GDP for the increase in refunds next year compared to last year. That’s a lot of cash,” he said.

However, he questioned whether this temporary boost would translate into increased hiring by businesses still grappling with uncertainty.

High interest rates present another significant challenge for small businesses, with many paying over 8% for working capital in an environment where inflation is trending toward 2.5%.

“That’s a very high real interest rate. So that’s a serious constraint on small business hiring,” he said, advocating for more aggressive Federal Reserve rate cuts beyond the one expected in the immediate future.

Shepherdson concluded by expressing concerns about potential margin compression for cyclical businesses due to tariffs, which he described as “a tax increase of about 1% of GDP.”

While acknowledging that the overall economy isn’t in “catastrophic shape,” he emphasized that certain sectors “aren’t working very well and could be working better,” adding that improvement would come “if the Fed cut faster.”

More on the U.S. economy

  • Initial jobless claims retreat to below 200K in Thanksgiving week
  • Market Voices: Bessent on tariff options; Pentagon's F/A-XX program
  • Bessent calls for change in how regional Fed presidents are chosen
  • Lutnick pushes back on tariff blame for negative ADP jobs print
  • ISM Services PMI rises more than expected in November

data.title: Salesforce posts impressive results but lands last in Dow 30 SA Quant Ratings
data.description:
Shares of Salesforce (CRM) are in the spotlight Thursday morning following the release of the customer relationship management giant’s latest earnings report.

The company may have delivered impressive results and upbeat guidance, but according to Seeking Alpha’s Quant Ratings, the stock ranks last among the 30 components of the Dow.  

Merck (MRK) tops the list with a Strong Buy rating of 4.87, while Chevron (CVX) follows with a Buy rating of 4.37.

The majority of Dow companies currently have a Hold rating according to Seeking Alpha’s Quant system. Tech megacaps like Nvidia (NVDA), Amazon (AMZN), and Apple (AAPL) all maintain Hold ratings in the 3.4-3.5 range, despite NVDA’s impressive +33.7% YTD performance and their trillion-dollar market capitalizations.

Here are all 30 Dow stocks ranked by their Seeking Alpha Quant Ratings:

data.title: Ciena ticks up as Morgan Stanley sees potential for guidance boost
data.description:
• Ciena (CIEN) shares rose 1.5% on Thursday as Morgan Stanley said it sees the potential for a boost to its guidance when it reports quarterly results on Dec. 11.
  • “We would expect CIEN to raise FY26 top-line estimates to ~19-20%, from the ~17% they laid out on the FQ3 call (which had been an increase from ~8-11% three year CAGR given in FQ4'24),” analyst Meta Marshall wrote in a note to clients. “This would lay the groundwork for FY26 to ultimately come in closer to 25% revenue growth, more in line with current buy side expectations.”
  • Marshall, who has an Equal-Weight rating on Ciena, but upped her price target to $185 from $140, said the company could also update its three-year outlook, something it hasn't done since the fourth-quarter of 2024.
  • “The company noted that they would be revising it higher [previously at 8-11% growth], but that it wasn't appropriate to do [it] early in the year given how strong demand had been,” Marshall added. “With Street currently embedding a ~15% CAGR for FY26 and 27 and buy side closer to 20-25%, we would expect them to give some bounds that allow the Street estimates to come up closer to where buy side estimates are.”
